pi_items.source: task.json description says 'Dr. Nathan Cruz'; setup_task.sh line 84 cleans WHERE first_name='Nathan'; export_result.sh line 25 queries WHERE first_name='Nathan'; vlm_checklist.json says 'Nathan Cruz'. Consistent across all files.
task.json description says 'Dr. Nathan Cruz'; setup_task.sh line 84 cleans WHERE last_name='Cruz'; export_result.sh line 25 queries WHERE last_name='Cruz'. Consistent across all files.

task.json description says 'email: ncruz@demoschool.edu'; vlm_checklist.json privileged_info says 'Email: ncruz@demoschool.edu'; export_result.sh queries staff.email column. Live DB schema confirms staff table has email varchar(100) column (evidence: 13_db_schemas.txt).

task.json description says 'Teacher profile'; staff table has profile varchar(30) column (evidence: 13_db_schemas.txt). user_profiles table confirms id=2 is 'teacher'/'Teacher' (evidence: direct_db_setup.sh line 86-89). Export queries staff.profile. Consistent.

task.json description says 'Code: APES101'; setup_task.sh line 75 cleans WHERE short_name='APES101'; export_result.sh line 28 queries WHERE short_name='APES101'. DB quirk: OpenSIS stores course code in courses.short_name column (varchar(25)), not a 'course_code' column. Live evidence: 05_clean_state.txt confirms 0 rows for APES101 after cleanup.
task.json description says 'AP Environmental Science'; courses table stores name in title column varchar(100) (evidence: 13_db_schemas.txt, 14_courses_credits.txt). Consistent across all files.

task.json description says 'Subject: Science'; setup_task.sh lines 113-115 seed 'Science'/'SCI' in course_subjects table. Live evidence: 04_prerequisites.txt confirms subject_id=1, title='Science', short_name='SCI' exists in DB. courses.subject_id references this table.

task.json description says 'Grade Level: 11'; courses table has grade_level decimal(10,0) column (evidence: 14_courses_credits.txt). Live evidence: 04_prerequisites.txt confirms school_gradelevels id=3 has short_name='11'. Students are seeded into grade 11 (evidence: 03_students_enrollment.txt shows grade_id=3, grade=11).

task.json description says 'Credits: 1.0'. DB QUIRK FOUND: The courses table does NOT have a credits column (evidence: 14_courses_credits.txt shows only: syear, course_id, subject_id, school_id, grade_level, title, short_name, rollover_id, last_updated, updated_by). Credits are stored on the course_periods table instead (credits decimal(10,3) column, evidence: 13_db_schemas.txt). The OpenSIS UI may show credits during course or section creation; the agent should enter 1.0 wherever the UI presents this field. Value is consistent with task intent.

task.json description says 'Period 3'; setup_task.sh lines 118-120 seed school_periods with title='Period 3', short_name='3', sort_order=3. Live evidence: 04_prerequisites.txt confirms period_id=1, title='Period 3', short_name='3'. DB QUIRK: The internal period_id is auto-incremented (1 in this case since it is the only period), but the display name is 'Period 3'. The agent selects by display name in the UI.

task.json description says 'Room 215'. DB NOTE: The course_periods DESCRIBE in evidence (13_db_schemas.txt) does not show a room column, but the existing assign_teacher_to_section task successfully uses room='Lab 1' in its setup_task.sh INSERT. This suggests the column exists but may not have been captured in the evidence dump. The agent enters '215' in whatever room field the OpenSIS UI presents during section configuration.

task.json description says 'seat limit of 20'; course_periods table has total_seats int(11) column (evidence: 13_db_schemas.txt). export_result.sh line 32 queries cp.total_seats. Existing tasks like set_section_seat_limit use this column successfully.

task.json description says 'assignment category called Assessments'; gradebook_assignment_types table has title varchar(100) column (evidence: 15_gradebook_schemas.txt). export_result.sh line 58 queries WHERE gat.title='Assessments'. Consistent.

task.json description says 'weighted at 50%'; gradebook_assignment_types.final_grade_percent column is decimal(6,5) (evidence: 15_gradebook_schemas.txt). The UI expects a percentage value; OpenSIS may store it as 0.50000 or 50.00000 depending on the PHP code. The agent enters 50 in the UI weight field.

task.json description says 'Baseline Assessment'; gradebook_assignments table has title varchar(100) column (evidence: 15_gradebook_schemas.txt). export_result.sh line 68 queries WHERE ga.title='Baseline Assessment'. Consistent.
task.json description says 'worth 100 points'; gradebook_assignments table has points decimal(10,0) column (evidence: 15_gradebook_schemas.txt). Consistent.

setup_task.sh line 156 inserts first_name='Olivia'. Live DB evidence: 03_students_enrollment.txt shows student_id=1, first_name='Olivia', last_name='Martinez', birthdate='2007-03-14', gender='Female', grade_id=3 (Grade 11), syear=2026, start_date=2025-08-15. Confirmed in running environment.
setup_task.sh line 156 inserts last_name='Martinez'. Live DB evidence: 03_students_enrollment.txt confirms last_name='Martinez'. Consistent across task.json, setup_task.sh, export_result.sh.

task.json description says 'Olivia Martinez (89)'. gradebook_grades.points column is decimal(6,2), so 89 would be stored as 89.00 (evidence: 15_gradebook_schemas.txt). export_result.sh line 73 queries gg.points. Value is the score the agent must enter in the gradebook UI.

setup_task.sh line 167 inserts first_name='Ethan'. Live DB evidence: 03_students_enrollment.txt shows student_id=2, first_name='Ethan', last_name='Park', birthdate='2007-07-22', gender='Male', grade_id=3 (Grade 11). Confirmed in running environment.
setup_task.sh line 167 inserts last_name='Park'. Live DB evidence: 03_students_enrollment.txt confirms last_name='Park'. Consistent across all files.
task.json description says 'Ethan Park (76)'. Same gradebook_grades.points column as above. Value is the score the agent must enter.

setup_task.sh line 187 inserts first_name='Sophia'. Live DB evidence: 03_students_enrollment.txt shows student_id=3, first_name='Sophia', last_name='Williams', birthdate='2007-11-05', gender='Female', grade_id=3 (Grade 11). Confirmed in running environment.
setup_task.sh line 187 inserts last_name='Williams'. Live DB evidence: 03_students_enrollment.txt confirms last_name='Williams'. Consistent across all files.
task.json description says 'Sophia Williams (92)'. Same gradebook_grades.points column. Value is the score the agent must enter.

task.json description says 'January 13, 2025'. setup_task.sh lines 87-101 clean attendance WHERE school_date='2025-01-13'. export_result.sh lines 87-95 query WHERE school_date='2025-01-13'. DB columns attendance_period.school_date and attendance_day.school_date are both DATE type (evidence: 16_attendance_schemas.txt). DATE QUIRK: The school year is dynamic (syear computed from current date). During March 2026 testing, syear=2026 means school year 2025-2026 (Aug 2025 to Jun 2026). The date 2025-01-13 falls BEFORE this school year. If OpenSIS validates date ranges, attendance entry for this date may be rejected. This is a known design consideration.

task.json description says 'Olivia as Present'. Live evidence: 17_attendance_codes_fixed.txt confirms attendance_codes id=1 is title='Present', short_name='P', state_code='H', default_code='Y'. The agent selects 'Present' in the UI; OpenSIS stores attendance_code=1 in attendance_period table.

task.json description says 'Ethan as Absent'. Live evidence: 17_attendance_codes_fixed.txt confirms attendance_codes id=2 is title='Absent', short_name='A', state_code='A'. The agent selects 'Absent' in the UI; OpenSIS stores attendance_code=2.
task.json description says 'Sophia as Present'. Same attendance code as Olivia (id=1, title='Present').

task.json metadata field. Used by verifier for pass/fail determination. Verifier is a stub (returns score=100 always); actual verification is via VLM checklist. Value is a design choice, not derived from data.

privileged_info_summary: This task requires a complete course lifecycle setup in OpenSIS v9.2. Login: admin / Admin@123. Step 1: Add staff member Nathan Cruz with email ncruz@demoschool.edu and Teacher profile (stored in staff table: first_name, last_name, email, profile columns). Step 2: Create course AP Environmental Science with code APES101 (stored as courses.short_name), subject Science (subject_id references course_subjects where title='Science'), grade level 11. Note: courses table has NO credits column; credits (1.0) are stored on course_periods.credits instead. Step 3: Create a course section (course_periods record) linked to the APES101 course, assigned to Period 3 (school_periods where title='Period 3', internal period_id=1 since it is the only period), with total_seats=20. Credits 1.0 goes on course_periods.credits. Step 4: Assign Nathan Cruz as teacher_id on the course_periods record. Step 5: Enroll three pre-existing 11th-grade students via the schedule table: Olivia Martinez (student_id auto-assigned, birthdate 2007-03-14, Female), Ethan Park (birthdate 2007-07-22, Male), Sophia Williams (birthdate 2007-11-05, Female). All three are confirmed in the DB with grade_id=3 (Grade 11) and proper student_enrollment records. Step 6: Create gradebook category 'Assessments' (gradebook_assignment_types.title) with final_grade_percent=50 (representing 50% weight). Step 7: Create assignment 'Baseline Assessment' (gradebook_assignments.title) with points=100, linked to the Assessments category via assignment_type_id. Step 8: Enter grades in gradebook_grades table: Olivia=89, Ethan=76, Sophia=92 (stored as points decimal(6,2)). Step 9: Record attendance for 2025-01-13 in attendance_period table: Olivia=Present (attendance_code=1), Ethan=Absent (attendance_code=2), Sophia=Present (attendance_code=1). Attendance codes are confirmed seeded: id=1 'Present' (P), id=2 'Absent' (A). The admin user has permissions for all required modules: scheduling/Courses.php, attendance/TakeAttendance.php, grades/Grades.php, students/Student.php, users/User.php. The export_result.sh produces valid JSON with 14 keys querying all verification targets. All data fields are empty before the agent starts (clean state confirmed). School year is dynamically computed from current date.
